home.morning: Morning's the best part. The water hasn't made up its mind yet.
Still water this early. Good time to show up.
The fish are already there, Grant. You just need to be still enough for them to notice you.
home.afternoon: Afternoon light hits the water different. Worth paying attention to.
Every pond has a secret corner. Take your time finding it.
The best casts aren't always the longest ones.
home.evening: End of the day, the water settles. Good time to think.
You caught something today even if the line stayed dry.
Still water remembers everything that happens above it.
adventure.start: Every fisherman has a first pond. Yours is right outside the door.
adventure.waiting: Still water remembers everything that happens above it. Be worth remembering.
The river already knows you're here. Now let it decide what to do about that.
A river tells you everything if you're quiet enough to hear it.
adventure.complete: You came. You cast. You paid attention. That's a good day.
You showed up. You paid attention. The river remembers that.
catch.first: The Bluegill was your first. You'll catch a hundred fish after this — but you'll always remember the first one that pulled back.
catch.bass: You gave the water your patience. It gave you this.
catch.any: There it is. I always figured you had it in you.
catch.none: The fish weren't ready. But you were there — that part counts.
Some days the water teaches more than the fish do.
journal.prompt: The journal holds more than what you caught — write what the day felt like.
Even one line is enough if it is true, Grant.
Let the page carry your memory. The lake remembers with you.
species.bluegill: Small and quick — the Bluegill was your first. That means something.
species.largemouth_bass: The big ones don't come easy. That's not bad news — that's the whole story.
species.channel_catfish: Hidden in the deep — the Catfish reminds us to look where others will not.
map.open: Every water on that map has something to teach. Some you haven't found yet.
fieldguide.open: Know where they rest and when they feed — meet them halfway.
fallback: The water has a way of speaking if we listen.
Show up, be quiet, and it'll come to you.
Still water remembers. Cast with presence.
